About the Programme

A Programme Designed for the Digital Age

The Digital Entrepreneur Academy at KLUST focuses on entrepreneurship, digital business, innovation, business information systems, digital marketing, e-commerce, and startup development. We prepare graduates to lead and innovate in an increasingly digital economy.

Programme Focus Areas
Entrepreneurship Digital Marketing E-Commerce Business Info Systems Social Media Innovation Mgmt Startup Development Business Analytics Knowledge Management Digital Leadership Tech Integration
Lecturer
SS
Mdm Suhaila Sard
Course: MGMT 3501 – Knowledge Management
Entry Requirements

Who Can Apply?

The Digital Entrepreneur Academy welcomes passionate individuals ready to build their future in digital business. Here's what you need to join us.

🎓
Route A

SPM / O-Level Leavers

  • ✓Minimum 5 credits in SPM including Bahasa Malaysia and Mathematics
  • ✓Pass in English (at least Grade B or equivalent)
  • ✓Foundation or pre-university qualification may be required
📜
Route B

Diploma / Foundation Holders

  • ✓Diploma in Business, IT, Commerce, or related field
  • ✓Minimum CGPA of 2.0 or equivalent
  • ✓Credit transfer may be available for relevant subjects

📋 Application Checklist

📄 Certified academic transcripts & certificates
🪪 Copy of MyKad / Passport
📸 Passport-sized photograph (recent)
✉️ Completed application form (online or physical)
💳 Application processing fee payment receipt
💡 Student Tip

"Don't stress too much about your grades — what matters most is your passion and willingness to learn. KLUST looks for motivated students who want to grow. I came in with just my diploma and honestly had the time of my life." — Sayidomar, Batch 2024

Ready to start your journey?
Applications are open year-round. Contact our admissions team for the next intake date.
Apply Now →
Why Choose Us

Built for Future-Ready Leaders

Eight compelling reasons why the Digital Entrepreneur Academy at KLUST is the right choice for your entrepreneurial journey.

🎯

Industry-Relevant Curriculum

Courses aligned with current digital business trends and employer expectations, updated regularly with industry input.

🛠

Hands-On Learning

Real projects, business simulations, and startup challenges that give you practical experience from day one.

💻

Digital Business Skills

Gain mastery in e-commerce, digital marketing, analytics, and the tools that power modern online businesses.

🚀

Entrepreneurship Training

Learn to ideate, validate, and launch your own venture with mentorship from experienced entrepreneurs.

👩‍🏫

Experienced Lecturers

Learn from academics and industry professionals who bring both theoretical depth and real-world expertise.

🏆

Career-Ready Graduates

Our graduates enter the workforce with the skills, confidence, and network to succeed immediately.

💡

Innovation & Startup Opportunities

Access to startup incubators, pitch competitions, and innovation labs to turn your ideas into reality.

🌐

Modern Learning Environment

State-of-the-art facilities, digital tools, and flexible online-offline hybrid learning for today's student lifestyle.

Course Structure

Core Modules & Subjects

A carefully designed curriculum covering every dimension of digital entrepreneurship and business innovation.

MODULE 01

Introduction to Entrepreneurship

Foundations of entrepreneurial thinking, opportunity recognition, and the startup mindset.

MODULE 02

Digital Marketing

SEO, SEM, content marketing, and data-driven campaign management across platforms.

MODULE 03

E-Commerce

Building, running, and optimising online stores, payment systems, and customer journeys.

MODULE 04

Business Information Systems

Enterprise software, data management, and information architecture for digital businesses.

MODULE 05

Social Media Marketing

Audience engagement, influencer strategies, and platform-specific content creation.

MODULE 06

Innovation Management

Design thinking, creative problem solving, and managing innovation processes in organisations.

MODULE 07

Startup Development

Lean startup methodology, MVP creation, funding, and scaling a new venture.

MODULE 08

Business Analytics

Using data, metrics, and analytics tools to make smarter business decisions.

MODULE 09

Knowledge Management

KM processes, knowledge workers, organisational learning, and knowledge systems. (MGMT 3501)

Student Experience

Campus Life at KLUST

Honest, real talk about what it's like being a student at the Digital Entrepreneur Academy — from campus vibes to the things no one tells you before you join.

☀️

A Typical Day

Morning lectures, group project sessions after lunch, and late-night study marathons before assignments are due. The days are full but never boring — there's always something new happening on campus.

🍜

Food & Hangout Spots

The campus café is where everyone gathers between classes. Nasi lemak for RM3.50, decent WiFi, and good company — it's basically our unofficial second classroom. The canteen gets loud at noon but the food is worth it.

💻

Facilities & Tech

We have access to computer labs with licensed software, a digital library, high-speed internet in most blocks, and a collaborative learning space perfect for group work and presentations.

🎉

Events & Activities

Pitch competitions, startup fairs, industry talks, and cultural festivals happen throughout the year. The entrepreneurship club runs regular events that let you network way before graduation.

From a Current Student

"What I Wish I Knew Before Joining KLUST"

📚
Start your assignments early
Lecturers assign projects that require real research. Don't leave them to the last minute — the content is deeper than most students expect.
🤝
Build your network from Day 1
Your classmates are your future business partners, referees, and colleagues. Treat every group project like a real startup opportunity.
🎯
Take KM seriously
MGMT 3501 sounds dry at first, but it genuinely changes how you think about organising and using information in business. It's one of the most useful subjects in the programme.
🌟
Use every resource available
From the library databases to the career centre to your lecturers' office hours — most students underuse these. The students who get ahead are the ones who ask questions.
— Sayidomar Abdinor Mohamed, Year 3, Digital Entrepreneur Academy

🗺️ Tips for New Students

  • 01Download the Moodle app — your digital lifeline for lectures, notes, and submissions
  • 02Join a student club in your first semester — the entrepreneurship and marketing clubs are the most active
  • 03Arrive 5 minutes early — parking near Block A fills up fast on lecture days
  • 04Set up your student email on day one — admin announcements go there first

📊 My Honest Experience

Coming in as a diploma student with a different background, I was nervous about keeping up. But the lecturers here — especially Mdm Suhaila in Knowledge Management — made the transition incredibly smooth. The class environment is collaborative, not competitive.


The assignments push you to think like an entrepreneur, not just a student. By Year 2 you're already building real digital projects. That's the kind of learning experience that actually prepares you for the real world.

Student Experience

BBA Majoring in Management

How We Apply Business Administration

The Digital Entrepreneur Academy delivers a Bachelor of Business Administration (BBA) with a major in Management — equipping students with the strategic, operational, and leadership skills to thrive in today's competitive business landscape.

🏛️
BBA CORE 01

Strategic Management

Students learn to analyse competitive landscapes, craft long-term organisational strategies, and lead business units through complex market environments using proven frameworks and real-world case studies.

  • SWOT, PESTLE & Porter's Five Forces analysis
  • Corporate strategy and competitive positioning
  • Strategic planning, goal-setting & execution
  • Mergers, acquisitions & business expansion
⚙️
BBA CORE 02

Operations Management

From supply chain logistics to process optimisation, students gain the tools to design and manage efficient business operations — reducing costs while improving quality and customer satisfaction.

  • Supply chain & logistics management
  • Quality control and process improvement (Lean, Six Sigma)
  • Resource planning and inventory management
  • Service operations and project management
👥
BBA CORE 03

Human Resource Management

Students explore talent acquisition, employee development, organisational behaviour, and workplace culture — building the people-management skills that define great business leaders.

  • Recruitment, selection & talent management
  • Performance appraisal and employee development
  • Organisational behaviour & team dynamics
  • Labour law, workplace ethics & diversity
💰
BBA CORE 04

Financial Management

Students gain essential financial literacy — from reading balance sheets and cash flow statements to making capital budgeting decisions and understanding corporate finance principles.

  • Financial statement analysis & reporting
  • Budgeting, forecasting & cost management
  • Capital structure and investment decisions
  • Business taxation and financial compliance
📣
BBA CORE 05

Marketing Management

From market research and consumer behaviour to brand strategy and digital campaigns, students learn to create and communicate value — driving customer acquisition and retention for organisations of any size.

  • Consumer behaviour & market segmentation
  • Brand management and positioning
  • Digital marketing, SEO & social media strategy
  • Product development and pricing strategies
🚀
BBA CORE 06

Entrepreneurship & Innovation

Students develop an entrepreneurial mindset — learning to identify opportunities, validate business ideas, secure funding, and build scalable ventures from the ground up in both digital and physical markets.

  • Business plan development & feasibility studies
  • Design thinking and innovation management
  • Startup funding — grants, investors & bootstrapping
  • Scaling operations and managing growth
🎓
Qualification
Bachelor of Business Administration (Hons)
📌
Major
Management
📅
Duration
3 Years Full-time
🏅
MQF Level
Level 6 — Accredited
📚
Credit Hours
120 Credit Hours
Website Purpose

Why This Website Exists

This website was created as a KM Final Assessment for MGMT 3501 — Knowledge Management at KLUST. It demonstrates how digital platforms can be used to organise, share, and apply knowledge in an educational context.

  • 01Help students understand entrepreneurship education at KLUST
  • 02Learn about digital business opportunities and career paths
  • 03Explore programme information clearly and accessibly
  • 04Prepare prospective students for future careers
  • 05Demonstrate KM processes through real digital application

Programme at a Glance

Target Audience SPM & Diploma Leavers
Study Mode Full-time & Part-time
Programme Level Bachelor's Degree
Duration 3 Years
Core Subject MGMT 3501 – KM
Institution KLUST
